title: Finance Administrator
description:
Finance Administrator



Hey! We’re Dilate, a result driven digital marketing agency with an impressive, diverse client list and a world-class, global team.

It’s our mission to do great marketing, for great businesses, ethically and in a way that benefits us all. We want our clients to win, their customers to win, and our employees to win.



We’ve got pretty ambitious growth plans and we’re looking for a Finance Administrator to join our team.



We are looking for someone highly detail-oriented, organised, and confident working with financial data. You’ll play a key role in keeping our financial operations running smoothly, with a strong focus on accuracy, compliance, and reporting.

This opportunity is part-time, 4 hours per day, 9am–1pm AWST, on a 6-month contract working with a team based in Perth, Western Australia.



So, the job.



* Perform regular and accurate Xero reconciliations, ensuring all transactions are correctly recorded and discrepancies are resolved.

* Manage sales invoicing and bill processing, including cross-checking retainers, payment terms, GST, and account codes.

* Maintain clean, organised, and up-to-date financial records for easy access and reporting.

* Prepare financial reports including cash flow, income statements, and balance sheets to support decision-making.

* Analyse financial data to identify trends, improvements, and cost-saving opportunities.

* Ensure compliance with financial regulations, company policies, and industry standards.

* Assist with audits and financial reviews, providing required documentation and support.

* Manage the finance inbox, ensuring timely responses and escalation where required.



Your skills.

* Proven experience as a Finance Administrator or similar role.

* Strong proficiency in Xero and Google Sheets (essential).

* Experience with Ignition and HubSpot (preferred).

* Solid understanding of financial principles and reporting.

* Excellent attention to detail and accuracy in data management.

* Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.

* Ability to work independently and meet deadlines efficiently.

* Effective communication skills and ability to collaborate across teams.

* Knowledge of financial regulations and compliance standards.



This is a great opportunity to play a key role in the financial health of a fast-growing agency.



If you feel that this is the job for you, we would love to hear from you!



Thanks for your time,

The Dilate Digital Team

title: Real Estate Media Quality Control & Delivery VA

WORK HOURS

 $4- $5 per hour based on experience

title: Legal Secretary
description:
Legal Assistant (Remote – International Applicants Welcome)

Location: Remote (International)

Position Type: Full-Time

Language Requirement: Fluent English required;

Experience Level: Legal assistant / paralegal experience preferred

 

About the Firm

We are a California-based law firm focused on consumer rights, employment law, personal injury, and civil litigation. Our practice is fast-paced, detail-oriented, and client-centered. We are seeking a reliable, highly organized Legal Assistant to support case management and administrative operations so our managing attorney can focus on strategy, advocacy, and firm growth.

This role is ideal for someone who is proactive, organized, and comfortable working remotely with U.S. court systems and deadlines.

 

Key Responsibilities

Client Intake & Communication

• Answer incoming phone calls and
s from clients, potential clients, and opposing counsel

• Screen new client inquiries and conduct initial intake calls using firm-provided scripts

• Monitor website intakes and promptly follow up to schedule intake or consultation calls

• Maintain regular communication with clients regarding case status and next steps

• Follow up with clients to obtain missing information or documents

• Maintain professionalism and confidentiality in all communications

Calendaring & Case Management

• Calendar court deadlines, hearings, mediations, depositions, and internal reminders

• Monitor upcoming deadlines and proactively flag urgent matters

• Maintain accurate case notes, task lists, and updates in the case management system



Client Onboarding & Engagement

• Prepare and send representation agreements using firm templates

• Prepare intake forms and ensure proper execution by clients

• Open new matters and organize digital client files

• Collect, organize, and label client documents (IDs, police reports, contracts, statements, etc.)

Drafting & Legal Support

• Draft simple pleadings, notices, and correspondence using firm templates

• Draft basic discovery (form discovery, RFAs, RFPs, simple interrogatories) under attorney direction

• Prepare service documents and coordinate service of process

• Draft and file proofs of service

Filing & Court Support

• E-file and mail-file documents in California state and U.S. federal courts

Mail & Service

 and similar services

Administrative & Operational Support

Preferred Qualifications

• Experience in consumer law, personal injury, or employment law

Important Notes

• Long-term opportunity with a growing firm

• Clear systems, templates, and training

title: Operations Director
description:
OPERATIONS DIRECTOR ROLE



Employee Retention Benefits, Inc. (ERB)



1. POSITION INFORMATION



Position Title: Operations Director 



Department: Operations / Client Experience 



Reports To: Integrator 



Location: Remote / Offshore (Philippines or equivalent) 







2. PURPOSE OF THE ROLE

The Operations Director is responsible for building, leading, and stabilizing ERB’s operational engine, with a primary focus on Client Service execution, internal workflows, and operational scalability.



This role ensures that all internal processes are structured, efficient, and consistently executed to support company growth.



The Operations Director owns how work gets done, ensuring that service delivery is predictable, scalable, and free from operational friction.



This is a builder and stabilizer role, not a maintenance role.



3. ROLE DEFINITION



Operations Director



Role Purpose 

The Operations Director owns Client Service (CST) execution, QA & Audit oversight (Public Sector), and internal workflow design. This role ensures that all operational processes are standardized, documented, and scalable.



The role is responsible for aligning execution across departments and ensuring that internal delivery meets company standards for accuracy, timeliness, and efficiency.



4. CORE AREAS OF OWNERSHIP



Client Service (CST execution & workflow) 

QA & Audit (Public Sector only) 

Operational capacity planning 

SOPs, systems, and documentation 

Cross-department delivery alignment 





5. KEY RESPONSIBILITIES



A. Operational Strategy & Execution



Design and own end-to-end operational workflows across Client Service and internal processes 

Translate leadership priorities into structured, executable workflows 

Ensure operational readiness to support company growth and increasing demand 

Identify inefficiencies, bottlenecks, and breakdowns — and implement permanent solutions 

B. QA & Audit Oversight (Public Sector Only)



Establish and enforce QA standards and audit checkpoints 

Own: 

defect prevention 

error tracking 

root-cause resolution 

Ensure compliance with internal SOPs and data accuracy standards 

Provide QA reporting and trend analysis to leadership 

Note: QA & Audit for Private Sector accounts remains under Account Director.



C. Client Service (CST) Execution Oversight



Own CST workflow structure and execution standards 

Ensure CST workflows align with account and enrollment requirements 

Maintain: 

SLA performance

service quality standards

escalation workflows

Drive process discipline and documentation standards

Eliminate recurring service issues through systemic fixes

Current Structure: CST and Operations function as a single execution team until further organizational scaling requires separation.







D. Team Build-Out & Leadership



Hire, onboard, and develop operational support roles 

Apply EOS LMA (Lead, Manage, Accountable) principles 

Define: 

clear role accountability 

KPIs and scorecards 

Coach tea
mbers to take ownership of outcomes 

E. Systems, SOPs & Scalability



Build and maintain SOPs in ConnecTeam and operational systems 

Standardize workflows for consistency and repeatability 

Ensure adoption and proper use of: 

CRM systems 

tracking tools 

reporting platforms 

Implement operational dashboards and scorecards 





F. Cross-Functional Alignment 



Partner with Finance to support: 

billing accuracy

payroll timing

operational capacity planning

Ensure smooth operational handoffs between departments 

Align internal workflows with business needs without creating friction

title: Pay-Per-Call Partner Recruiter — Debt Settlement
description:
I run a pay-per-call operation in the debt settlement vertical. My tracking software (Ringba) and buyer relationship are fully set up. The only thing I need is someone to find the people who already produce debt settlement phone calls and get them sending calls to my tracking number.



You are not generating leads. You are not making calls to consumers. You are not running a dialer or buying data.

Your job is B2B sales. You are recruiting call partners.



There are call centers, media buyers, and telemarketers around the world who ALREADY produce debt settlement warm transfer calls every day. They already have agents, data, dialers, and consumers on the phone. Right now they sell those calls for $15–$20 each. I will pay them $25–$30 per approved call — more than they're getting now.



Your job is to find those companies, pitch them my offer, close the partnership, and hand them a tracking number. When they say yes and start sending calls, you monitor quality and keep recruiting more.



Where you find them: 
, AffLift forum, pay-per-call
 groups, Upwork, 
, and cold outreach to call center operators.



Day-to-day: Send 20+ outreach messages per day. Follow up on conversations. Onboard new partners when they agree. Monitor call quality. Keep the pipeline growing.



This is a long-term role with growth potential. You start with one vertical (debt settlement). As we scale, you'll recruit partners for additional verticals — insurance, home services, solar, legal — using the exact same playbook.



Ideal background: BPO operations, affiliate marketing, performance marketing, outbound B2B sales, or partnership recruitment. If you've worked at a pay-per-call network, CPA network, or managed affiliate/publisher relationships, you're exactly who I'm looking for.



To apply, answer this one question in your message: "If I asked you to find 5 call centers that produce debt settlement warm transfers and pitch them a $25/call offer, where would you look first and what would you say?"



Wage/Salary: $700/month + $50 bonus per partner onboarded who produces approved calls



Desired No. of Hours Per Week: 20



Business or Contact Name: Ben Horch



Skills: Select Telemarketing, Lead Generation, and Outbound Sales if those options are available.





I will eventually raise the salary to $5,000 a month once I find the right person.

If you love design and enjoy experimenting with AI tools, this role is for you!



We are looking for someone who:

- Has strong Canva skills

- Is excited about creating AI images



ABOUT US



Young Publisher |

- We offer long-term roles

BENEFITS

title: Funnelish funnel builder/copywriter
description:
Hey there,



We are looking for a new tea
mber to join our fast-growing 6-figure eCommerce business.



We run multiple online stores across Europe and are scaling quickly. Because of this, we are expanding our team.



We are looking for someone responsible for one of the most important parts of our business:



Building high-converting sales funnels.



You will take products and turn them into full, branded offers that are ready to launch and convert.



WHAT YOU WILL DO



This includes:



- Product naming

- Logo creation

- Improving and refining copy

- Creating images that support the copywriting

- Structuring and building the full funnel

- Delivering a funnel that is ready to launch and convert



You will receive competitor funnels and ready-made templates as a base.



Your role is to take these and turn them into clean, well-structured, high-quality funnels that fit our standards.



This role requires speed, creativity, and strong attention to detail.



Meeting deadlines is extremely important.



4R MODEL - FUNNEL BUILDER



ROLE



- You are responsible for building complete, branded sales funnels

- You receive competitor funnels as inspiration and ready-made templates, then:

- Adapt them to our structure

- Refine and improve the copy where needed

- Create branding and visuals

- Deliver a polished funnel ready to launch



Your work directly supports our testing and scaling process.



RESPONSIBILITIES



- Work with competitor funnels as a starting point

- Adapt structure and copy to fit our templates and brand style

- Improve copywriting where needed to ensure clarity and flow

- Create a unique brand name and logo for each product

- Create images that support and strengthen the messaging

- Meet deadlines consistently

- Track working hours using our time tracking tool



RESULTS

- Funnels that meet our quality standards and KPIs

- Clean, professional branding that builds trust

- Clear, structured pages with strong flow

- Copy that supports conversions

- Consistent and reliable delivery

- Smooth collaboration with the team



REQUIREMENTS

- Experience with Funnelish or similar page builders

- Basic graphic design skills such as logo creation and image editing

- Ability to work with and adapt existing content

- Fluent in English

- Able to work independently with minimal supervision

- Organized, reliable, and deadline-driven

- Proactive communicator

- Plus: Experience in eCommerce or direct-response marketing



WHAT WE OFFER

- Full-time position, Monday to Friday, weekends off

- Excellent hourly pay 

- Bonus system linked to KPIs

- Time tracking for accurate payment

- Long-term role with growth opportunities

- Clear SOPs, templates, and competitor examples

- Fast-paced environment with strong learning opportunities



READY TO GROW WITH US?



If you enjoy structured creative work and want to be part of a fast-growing eCommerce team, we want to hear from you.



Please send:



Your resume

Samples of your work

title: Healthcare Intake & QA Coordinator
description:
DO NOT APPLY IF you do not meet the qualifications listed below.



Position Summary



We are seeking a highly organized and service-oriented Virtual On-Call Support Specialist to provide remote administrative and coordination support during after-hours, evenings, and weekends.

This role supports intake operations, scheduling follow-up, patient call management, customer service, care coordination assistance, QA support, and EMR documentation tasks.

The ideal candidate has prior healthcare experience, strong computer proficiency, excellent English communication skills (oral and written), and the ability to remain calm and professional during urgent situations.



Key Responsibilities



Intake & Administrative Support



• Assist with intake processing and referral coordination

• Upload and track physician orders in EMR

• Upload and monitor endorsement reports

• Ensure timely documentation processing and electronic filing

• Maintain organized and accurate digital records



Scheduling & Care Coordination



• Schedule and confirm follow-up visits

• Support communication between field clinicians and office staff

• Assist with care coordination activities

• Escalate urgent or clinical matters to appropriate leadership



Patient & Customer Service Support



• Answer and triage incoming patient and caregiver calls

• Provide professional, courteous, and compassionate customer service

• Accurately document all calls in EMR

• Route concerns appropriately and promptly



Quality Assurance (QA) Support



• Assist with documentation tracking and follow-up

• Support compliance monitoring processes

• Help ensure required documents are uploaded and complete

• Maintain organized electronic QA files



Qualifications & Requirements



Required



• Prior healthcare experience (home health, hospice, clinic, hospital, or medical office)

• Strong English language proficiency (oral and written)

• Excellent customer service skills

• Highly organized with strong attention to detail

• Strong computer skills and proficiency in Microsoft Office

• Ability to multitask and work independently

• Ability to work after-hours, evenings, and weekends (U.S. Pacific Time)

• Reliable high-speed internet and professional work environment



Preferred



• Home Health experience preferred

• EMR experience preferred

• Medical background (RN, LVN, Medical Assistant, or allied health) preferred

• Clinical licensure preferred





How to Apply



• Please send a short cover letter highlighting your experience relevant to the role you are applying for, and include your answers to the questions below:

1. Please describe your healthcare experience in detail. Include the setting (home health, hospice, clinic, hospital, etc.), your role, how long you worked there, and the specific administrative or coordination tasks you handled.

2. How many years of experience do you have specifically in Home Health?

3. How many years of experience do you have within Home Health?

4. How many years of experience do you have scheduling Home Health clinicians?

5. What Home Health EMR systems have you used (e.g., Kinnser/WellSky, Homecare Homebase, Axxess, etc.)?

6. Describe your experience handling intake and patient calls in Home Health.

title: Sales Development Representative (SDR) – High-Volume Cold Calling (B2G)
description:
Remote / Work From Home | Company based in California

Full-Time | Remote | CST Hours



About Us

Bi-Rite Global is a certified small business and a trusted retailer that supplies a wide range of products to government agencies — including federal, state, and education sectors. Based in California, we are rapidly expanding and focused on delivering quality products, competitive pricing, and reliable service to our government partners.



Role Summary

We are looking for a Sales Development Representative (SDR) – High-Volume Cold Calling (B2G) that is responsible for initiating outbound calls to federal, state, and local government agencies to identify contracting opportunities, introduce company services, and qualify decision-makers involved in procurement. The role supports the business development pipeline by generating leads, setting capability briefings, and gathering intelligence on upcoming bids and purchasing needs.



Key Responsibilities

• Conduct high-volume outbound calls to government agency front desks and effectively navigate phone trees to reach relevant departments

• Ask targeted, strategic questions to identify key procurement or purchasing decision-makers (by name and role)

• Introduce company capabilities and differentiate services

• Qualify opportunities based on agency needs, budgets, and timelines

• Schedule capability briefings or discovery meetings for sales/business development teams

• Gather information on upcoming solicitations, RFPs, and purchasing cycles

• Accurately capture and log contact details (name, title,
, direct phone line) into a CRM or tracking spreadsheet

• Professionally handle gatekeepers and objections, including situations where information is not readily disclosed

• Maintain organized and up-to-date contact lists to support ongoing outreach efforts

• Track targets such as contacts reached, meetings set, and opportunities generated



Role Focus

• This position is primarily focused on contact discovery and list building, not full-cycle sales or closing.



Qualifications 

• Professional phone presence and clear communication skills

• Persistent and comfortable with high-volume outbound calling and rejection

• Strong research skills to identify government decision-makers

• Organized with accurate CRM documentation and time management

• Understanding of B2G (Business to Government) sales or ability to learn quickly

• Experience in lead generation, appointment setting, telemarketing, or inside sales

• Exposure to government procurement or public-sector contracting preferred

• Familiarity with RFP/RFQ processes, vendor registrations, and contract vehicles is an advantage

• Able to clearly present company capabilities and services



Work Environment

• 100% Remote (must have reliable work-from-home setup)

• Structured schedule aligned with business hours of government agencies

• Use of approved scripts and compliance-focused communication

• Calls primarily to public offices, vendors, or registered partners

• Performance tracked by call quality, accuracy, and documentation — not aggressive contracting

• Collaboration with compliance, procurement, and contracts teams

• Strict adherence to data privacy, confidentiality, and record-keeping standards

• Professional and formal communication style required

• This is a full time position



Technical Requirements

• Stable internet (minimum 50 Mbps download/upload on fiber connection)

• Backup internet (LTE mobile data or pocket Wi-Fi)

• Laptop or desktop with Intel i5 equivalent or higher, 8GB RAM

• Quality headset with noise-canceling microphone

• Functional webcam



What’s Next

Send your application with the subject line:  “I want this job – SDR”



Include:

1. A 2-minute introduction Loom video about yourself and your cold calling experience.

2. Attach your Loom URL, resume, and any relevant experience samples in this form (
). 



Important: Only candidates who follow instructions will be considered.

title: Post Production Specialist
description:

mbers to maintain workflow efficiency.

title: E-commerce Operations Rockstar – Online Gaming Retailer
description:
Want to be part of the future of gaming retail?

Are you highly self-motivated, detail-oriented, and a bit of a tech detective? We are looking for a Product & Operations VA to join our team at our online gaming retail shop. This isn't just "typing data": it’s about managing the pulse of our online stores across WooCommerce, Amazon, Shopee, Lazada, Carousell and TikTok.



The Mission:

We are a growing gaming retail company. Your work will involve everything from hunting down product specs for the latest ASUS gear to managing price health and keeping our social media buzzing.



What You’ll Be Doing:

- The Hunt: Source product data/images from Amazon, Play-Asia, GameFly, and manufacturer sites. No links provided, you find the best info!

- Store Management: Data entry, content verification and data migration between platforms.

- Pricing & Stock Ninja: If a supplier drops a price by $30, you're the one updating our store to stay competitive. If they restock, you're reactivating our listings.

- AI-Powered Socials: Use AI tools to write and schedule engagement-triggering posts.

- Problem Solving: Handling "Listing Errors" (e.g., if TikTok rejects a brand, you find the workaround)

- Operations: Order processing and occasional customer service when needed.



The "Tech-Forward" Catch:

We are obsessed with efficiency. To help us build better tools for you, you will live-stream your screen via a private YouTube link during your shift. This helps us identify bottlenecks and eventually build automation that makes your job easier and more focused on high-level verification rather than manual typing.



What You Need:

- The Beast: A PC with at least 16GB RAM (Non-negotiable).

- The Connection: Fast Fiber + 5G Backup for when the power/internet dips.

- The Brain: Self-motivated, meticulous, and an independent learner. Gaming interest is a HUGE plus.

- The Commitment: 48 hours/week. Monday to Saturday.



Salary: $450 USD / Month (Fixed), no 13th month. Paid bi-weekly via Wise.



How to Apply: I do not check DMs. Please apply strictly via this Google Form: gms.hm/vahire26

title: Commercial Property Operations Coordinator
description:
******TO BE CONSIDERED FOR THIS ROLE, YOU MUST SUBMIT YOUR RESUME AND VIDEO INTRODUCTION.****** 



Commercial Property Operations Coordinator



Job Type: Full-Time

Work Hours: Monday to Friday, 9:00 AM to 6:00 PM Pacific Time, with a 1-hour break

Salary: $1100 per month



Job Overview:

We are seeking a detail-oriented and highly organized Commercial Property Operations Coordinator to support a commercial real estate portfolio of retail and office properties by overseeing maintenance tracking, vendor coordination, compliance monitoring, lease administration support, and day-to-day operational follow-ups. This role works closely with the onshore property management team to ensure tasks are properly tracked, vendors are consistently followed up with, timelines are met, and nothing falls through the cracks during high-volume periods. Spanish-speaking ability is required to effectively communicate with Spanish-speaking vendors.



Key Responsibilities:



* Maintenance & Work Order Tracking

Maintain a master log of maintenance and repair items across commercial properties

Track work orders from scheduling through completion and invoicing

Follow up with vendors and onsite staff to confirm timelines and status

Flag delays, non-responses, or repeat issues for escalation

Maintain clear status categories (requested, scheduled, in progress, completed, invoiced)



* Vendor Coordination & Follow-Up

Send bid requests and RFPs using approved templates

Track incoming proposals and follow up on outstanding bids

Coordinate vendor scheduling and access after vendor selection

Collect close-out documentation (photos, completion confirmation, invoices)

Assist with vendor onboarding documentation (W-9s, COIs, licenses)



* Administrative & Compliance Support

Answer office phone

Track routine inspections, testing, and compliance items (fire/life safety, elevator, backflow, etc.)

Assist with COI tracking for tenants and vendors

Support documentation requests from tenants, vendors, lenders, and internal team

Maintain organized digital files and trackers for commercial properties

Assist with utility account transfers and service changes as directed



* Lease & Property Administration (Non-Legal)

Maintain lease abstract summaries (critical dates, options, notice deadlines)

Track upcoming renewals, options, and notice requirements

Prepare routine notices using approved templates for review

Maintain updated tenant contact lists and
 distribution lists.



* Task Tracking & Operational Support

Track open items and ensure follow-up

Send reminders and status updates to keep projects moving

Support multiple concurrent projects by maintaining timelines and next steps

Provide weekly status summaries of open items and priorities



Required Skills & Qualifications:



2–4 years of experience supporting commercial property management or real estate operations

Spanish language proficiency (spoken) is preferred to support vendor coordination

Familiarity with maintenance workflows, vendor coordination, and property documentation

Strong organizational and follow-up skills

Excellent written English and professional communication skills

Comfortable working with spreadsheets, trackers, and task management tools

Detail-oriented, reliable, and comfortable working behind the scenes

Able to manage multiple tasks and follow up consistently without dropping items



Note: An NBI or police clearance will be required upon acceptance of the job offer.

title: Senior AWS DevOps Engineer – AWS Cost Optimization & Kubernetes
description:
Job Description:

About the Role

We're a blockchain/DeFi company running production infrastructure that's bleeding money unnecessarily. Our AWS bill is sitting at $4,500/month – and we know it shouldn't be anywhere near that. We need someone who can come in, audit the whole setup, and actually fix it. Not just give us a report that sits in a folder.



We've already been through a few candidates who talked a big game but couldn't execute. We're done with that. If you're the type who needs handholding or can't work independently, this isn't for you.





What You'll Actually Do

- Audit our entire AWS infrastructure and identify exactly where we're wasting money (we expect significant savings)

- Optimize our Kubernetes clusters – right-size nodes, fix resource requests/limits, implement autoscaling that actually works

- Implement cost controls: Reserved Instances, Savings Plans, spot instances where appropriate, and cleanup of orphaned resources

- Set up monitoring and alerts so costs don't creep back up without us knowing

- Document everything you do so the team can maintain it





Hard Requirements (Non-Negotiable)

- 3+ years hands-on Kubernetes experience in production environments – EKS specifically preferred

- Deep AWS knowledge: EC2, EKS, RDS, S3, CloudWatch, Cost Explorer, Trusted Advisor

- Proven track record of actually reducing AWS costs (be ready to share numbers/percentages)

- Strong scripting skills (Python, Bash) for automation

- Infrastructure as Code: Terraform or CloudFormation (Terraform preferred)

- Self-starter who can take ownership and execute without constant oversight





!!!!CRITICAL: This Role Requires Surgical Precision

This is production infrastructure running live systems. Think of this like surgery – one wrong move can take everything down.

- NO cowboy moves. You don't delete, modify, or remove anything without a clear plan first.

- Every change must be planned out, documented, and confirmed with the CEO before execution.

- Extreme attention to detail. You double-check and triple-check before touching anything.

- If you're not 100% sure what something does, you ask first. You don't assume.

- Methodical execution only. We value careful and correct over fast and reckless.





Strong Plus

- Experience with blockchain/Web3 infrastructure

- Karpenter, Cluster Autoscaler, or KEDA experience

-  AWS certifications (Solutions Architect, DevOps Engineer)

- Experience with FinOps tools (Kubecost, CloudHealth, 
)





What We Need In Your Application

Don't send a generic application. We will ignore it. Include:

- Specific examples of AWS cost optimization you've done – what was the setup, what did you change, what % did you save?

- Your Kubernetes experience – cluster sizes you've managed, scaling strategies you've implemented

- Your availability and timezone (we need someone who can overlap with US business hours for some calls)





Compensation & Structure

- $20-25/hour based on experience and proven results

- 20 hours/week to start – potential for more hours for the right person

- Fully remote

- Initial project: Comprehensive audit + implementation plan with clear cost reduction targets





If you're someone who can actually deliver results and not just talk about them – we want to hear from you. Show us what you've done.

title: Group Manager - Operations (Travel)
description:
Urgently hiring for one of our direct clients in the Philippines.



Job Title: Group Manager - Operations (Travel)

Location: Bridgetown / Eastwood

Work Mode: Onsite, Shifting Schedule



Note: Candidate should have 2-3 Years of leadership experience.



Required: 

• CRYPTIC/NATIVE GDS EXPERIENCE IS REQUIRED

• MUST HAVE RECENT/CURRENT EXPERIENCE IN TRAVEL ACCOUNT

• IATA/ UFTAA Level -1 certified. (Preferred)

title: Video Editor
description:
******TO BE CONSIDERED FOR THIS ROLE, YOU MUST SUBMIT YOUR RESUME AND VIDEO INTRODUCTION.******



Video Editor



Job Type: Part-Time

Work Hours: Monday to Friday, 1:00 PM to 5:00 PM EST

Salary: $450 per month



Job Overview: 

We’re seeking a creative and detail-oriented Video Editor to join our content studio. You’ll be editing short-form videos for social media and collaborating on influencer marketing campaigns. If you’re passionate about storytelling through visual content and thrive in a fast-paced, creative environment, this role is for you.



Key Responsibilities:

Edit and polish short-form videos (15 s–2 min) tailored for platforms like TikTok, Instagram Reels, YouTube Shorts,
, LinkedIn

Integrate motion graphics, captions, music, sound effects

Collaborate with content creators and influencer teams to bring creative concepts to life

Manage timelines, draft rough cuts, and refine videos based on stakeholder feedback

Ensure all videos adhere to brand guidelines, quality standards, and platform best practices

Stay up to date on social video trends and recommend new editing approaches



Qualifications:

Proficiency in Adobe Premiere Pro (required); experience with After Effects or similar tools is a plus

2+ years of experience editing short-form, social-first content

Strong eye for pacing, storytelling, and visual design

Ability to format videos for various platforms (9:16, 1:1, 4:5, etc.)

Experience integrating motion graphics, transitions, captions, and audio

Excellent communication skills and responsiveness to feedback

Highly organized and able to manage multiple projects under tight deadlines

Prior experience with influencer content or marketing campaigns is highly valued

Familiarity with mobile editing tools (e.g., Descript, Premiere Rush) is beneficial

Basic design skills, such as creating thumbnails or light animations, are a plus. 



Note: An NBI or police clearance will be required upon acceptance of the job offer.

title: Experienced GHL Specialist – Build Scalable Systems & Automations
description:
Milkyway Is Hiring



Are you the systems-driven GHL Specialist we’re looking for?



Are you highly organised, technically confident in GoHighLevel, and obsessed with building systems that actually work—not just look good on the surface?



Do you enjoy designing clean automations, optimising customer journeys, and turning messy processes into scalable systems?

If you’re someone who can combine structured thinking, technical execution, and smart use of AI to improve performance—not just speed—this could be the role for you.



About Milkyway

Milkyway is a digital agency helping businesses grow through strategy, branding, websites, content, and performance marketing.

Behind every high-performing campaign is a well-built system—and that’s where you come in.

We’re growing quickly and are now looking for a dedicated GHL (GoHighLevel) Specialist to join our team.



ABOUT YOU

The ideal person takes ownership of systems, thinks beyond tasks, and builds automation that supports real business outcomes—not just technical completion.



You are:

• Highly experienced with GoHighLevel (GHL). (Do not apply if you are a beginner.)  

• Confident building workflows, automations, funnels, campaigns, and dashboards end-to-end.  

• Strong in mapping and improving customer journeys across marketing and sales pipelines.  

• Exceptionally organised and detail-oriented.  

• Comfortable troubleshooting system, automation, and data issues independently.  

• Process-driven with strong logical thinking.  

• Proactive in identifying inefficiencies and improving systems before issues arise.  

• Calm under pressure and solutions-focused.  

• A clear communicator who can explain systems in simple, practical terms.  

• Accountable, reliable, and consistent in execution.  

• A fast learner who stays up to date with GHL updates and best practices.  

• At least 2 years of experience working in an agency environment.



AI + SYSTEMS CAPABILITY

We’re looking for someone who understands both traditional system building and modern AI-powered workflows.

You should:

• Be able to design and build systems without relying on AI.  

• Know how to use AI tools inside and outside GHL to enhance workflows, speed, and insights.  

• Have experience working with GHL AI features (including AI agents and voice agents).  

• Be able to refine, structure, and validate AI outputs—not just deploy them.  

• Understand where AI improves efficiency—and where manual logic and control are still required.  

If you rely on AI without understanding the underlying system logic, this role is not a fit.



ABOUT THE ROLE

The GHL Specialist reports directly to the CEO and works closely with the wider team across marketing, sales, and client delivery. You will:

• Build, manage, and optimise CRM systems inside GoHighLevel.  

• Create and maintain workflows, triggers, and automations.  

• Build and manage SMS and
 campaigns.  

• Create and optimise funnels and landing pages within GHL.  

• Manage integrations between GHL and third-party tools.  

• Monitor system performance and proactively troubleshoot issues.  

• Maintain clean pipelines, accurate contact data, and reporting dashboards.  

• Build and optimise GHL AI agents and voice agents.  

• Support onboarding of new clients into structured GHL systems.  

• Document processes and ensure systems are scalable and repeatable.  

• Create and maintain snapshots based on validated systems and workflows.  



WORK SETUP

This is a remote role, ideally suited to someone working within or close to Australian business hours.

We’re looking for someone who wants to be an integral part of a growing agency—not just complete tasks, but help build the systems behind our growth.

It needs to be a genuine yes from both sides.



WHAT YOU’LL GET

A supportive, growth-focused environment where your systems expertise makes a real impact.



• A collaborative and professional team culture  

• Clear ownership over systems and automation  

• Structured onboarding and training aligned with our standards  

• Opportunities to deepen your expertise in automation and CRM strategy  

• Exposure to a wide range of client accounts and use cases  

• Flexibility and trust in how you manage your work  

We believe strong systems are the foundation of sustainable growth—for our clients and our team.



HOW TO APPLY

If this role sounds like a strong fit, we’d love to hear from you.



Send an
 to apply and Include the word “Stardust” creatively in your application so we know you’ve read this in full.  

Add a link to your portfolio and resume. Make sure to include 3 professional references in your resume. 



If you know someone who would be a great fit, feel free to pass this opportunity along.

We look forward to meeting the GHL Specialist who will help power the systems behind our growth.

title: Content Specialist -Help Scale Brands Through High-Impact Content
description:
Are you the creative and strategic Content Specialist we’re looking for?

Are you a strong writer who can turn ideas into clear, compelling content that actually performs and not just reads well? Do you enjoy blending strategy, data, and creativity to drive real business results?



If you take pride in your craft, think beyond just writing, and know how to use both traditional content skills and modern AI tools to produce better outcomes, this could be the role for you.



About Milkyway

Milkyway is a digital agency helping businesses grow through strategy, branding, websites, content, and digital marketing. We work closely with our clients to bring clarity to their messaging and consistency to how they show up online.



We’re growing quickly and are now looking for a Content Specialist to join our team.



ABOUT YOU

The ideal person wants to grow with us, take ownership of content quality, and elevate how our clients communicate.

You are:

• A confident writer with strong attention to detail and structure.  

• Comfortable creating content across websites, social media,
 marketing, SEO, and ads.  

• Strategic—you understand that content exists to drive outcomes, not just fill space.  

• Organised and able to manage content calendars and deadlines independently.  

• Experienced in maintaining and adapting brand voice across different platforms.  

• Familiar with SEO principles and writing for search intent.  

• Curious about performance and motivated to improve content based on data.  

• Proactive with ideas and comfortable contributing in planning sessions.  

• Accountable, reliable, and consistent in delivering high-quality work.  

• At least 2 years of experience working in an agency environment.



AI + Content Capability 

We’re looking for someone who:

• Has strong foundational writing skills without relying on AI.  

• Knows how to use AI tools (e.g. ChatGPT or similar on a professional set-up) to enhance speed, research, ideation, and optimisation—not replace thinking.  

• Can refine, edit, and elevate AI-generated outputs into high-quality, on-brand content.  

• Understands when to use AI—and when not to.  



If you rely entirely on AI to write for you, this role is not a fit. If you know how to leverage AI as a tool while maintaining quality and originality, you’ll thrive here.



ABOUT THE ROLE

The Content Specialist reports directly to the CEO and works closely with the wider team to support marketing and growth initiatives you will:

• Create high-quality website content, blog posts, landing pages, and SEO content.  

• Write engaging social media and
 marketing content aligned to brand voice.  

• Develop ad copy to support paid campaigns across multiple platforms.  

• Plan and manage content calendars across channels.  

• Maintain and apply brand style guides consistently.  

• Review and optimise existing content for clarity, performance, and SEO.  

• Analyse content performance and provide insights and recommendations.  

• Support campaign launches with coordinated content across channels.  

• Collaborate with designers, web developers, and marketing specialists.  



WORK SETUP

This is a remote role, ideally suited to someone who can work within or close to Australian business hours.

We’re looking for someone who wants to be part of a growing agency and contribute meaningfully to client success. It needs to be a genuine yes from both sides.



WHAT YOU’LL GET

A supportive, growth-focused environment where your writing and ideas genuinely matter.

• A collaborative and professional team culture  

• Clear systems and processes to support high-quality work  

• Training and onboarding to help you succeed in the role  

• Opportunities to develop your skills across content, SEO, and strategy  

• Exposure to a wide range of industries and content types  

• Flexibility and trust in how you manage your work  

We believe great content drives great outcomes—for our clients and our team.



HOW TO APPLY

If this role sounds like a strong fit, we’d love to hear from you.



Include the word “Stardust” creatively in your application so we know you’ve read this in full.  

Add a link to your portfolio and resume. Make sure to include 3 professional references in your resume. 



If you know someone who would be a great fit, feel free to pass this opportunity along.

We look forward to meeting the Content Specialist who will help take our clients’ messaging and performance to the next level.

title: Gaming Tutorial Gameplay Recorder
description:
We are looking for a Gaming Tutorial Gameplay Recorder to capture clear, step?by?step in?game footage for “how to” tutorials. You will follow provided instructions, record the required actions in the game, and deliver raw video files to our team. We handle all editing.



Responsibilities

Follow written or video instructions to reproduce specific “how to” steps in?game.



Record smooth, high?quality gameplay footage while performing the required actions.



Ensure all key steps are clearly shown on screen and in the correct order.



Capture multiple takes if needed to ensure clean, usable footage.



Name and organise files correctly and upload them to our shared drive or platform.



Communicate any issues with game performance, access, or tasks to the team.



Strong gaming PC capable of running the required games smoothly while recording (dedicated GPU required).



Reliable high?speed internet connection for downloading games/updates and uploading large video files.



Experience using gameplay capture tools (e.g., OBS, NVIDIA ShadowPlay, AMD ReLive, or similar).



Ability to carefully follow step?by?step instructions and replicate them in?game.



Access to a quiet environment to avoid background distractions in recordings (even if no commentary is needed).



Good communication skills and ability to meet deadlines. Wages will be negotiable.

title: Sales Manager (Remote - Part Time)
description:
Sales Manager (Remote – Part Time, Inbound Sales Focus)



Position Overview



You own sales performance from first contact through signed work.



Your job is to take inbound leads generated by our marketing 
, social media, Houzz, phone calls,
), respond quickly, qualify the right opportunities, move deals forward, and close profitable projects.



This is a sales role focused on closing and pipeline management. This is NOT a marketing role.



You are responsible for turning inbound demand into signed work while protecting company standards, capacity, and client experience.



This role starts part time with the opportunity to grow into a full-time position based on results.



What Success Looks Like



*All inbound leads are responded to quickly and professionally

*Qualified opportunities move steadily through the pipeline

*Close rate stays at 25%+ on qualified leads

*The pipeline stays organized, active, and up to date

*Signed work matches the type of projects we want more of



Key Responsibilities

Inbound Lead Management and Qualification



*Own all inbound leads from
, social media, Houzz, phone calls, and

*Respond quickly and move each lead into the next step

*Qualify prospects on budget, timeline, project type, and readiness

*Disqualify poor-fit leads to protect time and resources

*Ensure no lead is ignored, lost, or left without follow-up



Sales Process and Closing



*Own the sales process from first conversation to signed agreement

*Lead discovery calls, consultations, and follow-ups

*Handle objections and guide prospects toward decisions

*Keep deals moving and do not let opportunities go cold

*Clearly communicate scope, pricing, and next steps

*Ask for the sale and close qualified opportunities



Proposal and Deal Management



*Coordinate with the Owner on pricing and scope

*Present proposals clearly and confidently

*Capture objections, changes, and decision factors

*Ensure clients understand what is included and what is not



Pipeline Ownership



*Track every opportunity and its next step

*Keep pipeline updated daily

*Re-engage stalled opportunities

*Maintain full visibility on deal status at all times



Handoff to Production



*Hand off sold projects with complete notes and expectations

*Ensure alignment between what was sold and what will be delivered

*Communicate client priorities, timeline, and concerns clearly



Daily / Weekly Expectations

Daily



*Respond to new inbound leads

*Follow up with active prospects

*Move deals forward

*Update pipeline

*Communicate key updates to Owner



Weekly



*Review pipeline and deal stages

*Report on lead volume, conversions, and signed work

*Re-engage stalled opportunities

*Confirm next steps for all active deals



Please answer this form to officially apply:

title: IT Specialist (Microsoft 365 & End-User Support)
description:
IT SPECIALIST ROLE 



Employee Retention Benefits, Inc. (ERB) 



1. POSITION INFORMATION 



Position Title: IT Specialist (Microsoft 365 & End-User Support)  



Department: Technology / Operations Support  



Reports To: Integrator (or Operations Director, if delegated)  



Location: Remote / Offshore  



 



2. PURPOSE OF THE ROLE 



The IT Specialist is responsible for maintaining reliable day-to-day technology operations across ERB, ensuring that all users have secure, uninterrupted access to systems, tools, and platforms required to perform their roles. 



This role owns end-user support, Microsoft 365 administration, and endpoint/security management, ensuring that the organization operates efficiently, securely, and without unnecessary technical disruption. 



The IT Specialist acts as both a technical support function and a systems enabler, minimizing downtime and improving the overall employee technology experience. 



 



3. ROLE DEFINITION 



IT Specialist (Microsoft 365 & End-User Support) 



Role Purpose 

The IT Specialist provides hands-on technical support and manages core IT systems, with a focus on Microsoft 365, user lifecycle management, and endpoint security. 



This role ensures that all users are properly onboarded, supported, secured, and equipped with the tools needed to operate effectively in a remote environment. 



 



4. CORE AREAS OF OWNERSHIP 



End-user IT support (hardware, software, access)  



Microsoft 365 administration and environment management  



User onboarding and offboarding lifecycle  



Security and endpoint management (Intune, Defender, MFA)  



IT systems documentation and support processes  



 



5. KEY RESPONSIBILITIES 



A. End-User IT Support 



Provide daily technical support across:  



hardware and devices  



software and applications  



 and access-related issues  



Troubleshoot and resolve issues across:  



Windows  



macOS  



Support Microsoft Office and common business tools  



Assist users with Microsoft 365 applications:  



Outlook  



Teams  



OneDrive  



SharePoint  



Deliver timely remote support to minimize user downtime  



 



B. Microsoft 365 Environment Management 



Administer Microsoft 365 users, licenses, and access controls  



Configure and maintain:  



Exchange Online  



Microsoft Teams  



OneDrive  



SharePoint  



Manage:  



mailboxes  



permissions  



security settings  



Monitor Microsoft 365 service health and respond to issues  



Optimize license allocation to ensure cost efficiency  



 



C. User Onboarding & Offboarding 



Create and configure new user accounts  



Assign appropriate system access and licenses  



Set up:  



 accounts  



multi-factor authentication (MFA)  



required applications  



Support device setup and user configuration  



Execute secure offboarding:  



deactivate accounts  



revoke access  



ensure data security compliance  



 



D. Security & Endpoint Management 



Implement and maintain security controls, including:  



MFA  



Conditional Access policies  



password policies  



Monitor sign-in activity and respond to risk alerts  



Manage and configure:  



Microsoft Intune (device compliance, app deployment)  



Microsoft Defender for Endpoint (threat detection and response)  



Data Loss Prevention (DLP) policies  



Assist in investigation and remediation of suspicious or high-risk activity  



 



E. Systems Documentation & Process Support 



Maintain IT documentation, SOPs, and troubleshooting guides  



Standardize onboarding/offboarding processes  



Support internal teams with system access and tool configuration  



Ensure consistency in IT support workflows and resolution standards  



 



6. REPORTING & ALIGNMENT STRUCTURE 



Direct Report 



Integrator (or Operations Director if IT is nested under Operations)  



Works Closely With 



Operations Director (workflow and system alignment)  



HR (onboarding/offboarding coordination)  



Finance (license cost control and system access governance)  



All departments (end-user support)  



 



7. AUTHORITY & BOUNDARIES 



? Authority 



Owns end-user IT support and issue resolution  



Owns Microsoft 365 environment administration  



Owns user access, permissions, and system configurations  



Owns enforcement of endpoint and identity security policies  



 



? Boundaries 



Does not own business systems strategy (defined by leadership)  



Does not implement major system changes without approval  



Does not override department-specific workflows or tool ownership  



Does not act as a software engineering or development role  



 



8. KPIs & SCORECARD (EOS-ALIGNED) 



The IT Specialist is measured on: 



Average response and resolution time for IT support tickets  



System uptime and availability (Microsoft 365 and core tools)  



User onboarding/offboarding completion accuracy and timeliness  



Security compliance (MFA, device compliance, access control)  



Number of recurring IT issues (trend reduction)  



License utilization efficiency and cost optimization  



 



9. WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE 



Users experience minimal downtime and fast issue resolution  



Onboarding and offboarding processes are seamless and secure  



Microsoft 365 environment is stable, organized, and optimized  



Security risks are proactively managed and minimized  



IT issues are resolved systematically, not repeatedly  



Employees have confidence in IT support and systems reliability  



 



10. SUPPORT MODEL & EXPECTATIONS 



Full-time, remote IT support role  



Availability during standard business hours  



Responsible for both:  



reactive support (issue resolution)  



proactive support (system monitoring and improvements)  



 



11. IDEAL CANDIDATE PROFILE 



Proven experience in IT support or systems administration  



Strong knowledge of Microsoft 365 administration  



Experience supporting Windows and macOS environments  



Familiarity with:  



Microsoft Intune  



Microsoft Defender  



IT security best practices  



Strong troubleshooting, communication, and documentation skills  



Ability to work independently in a remote environment  



 



12. PREFERRED / BONUS EXPERIENCE 



Experience with GoHighLevel (GHL) administration or integrations  



Familiarity with 
 or similar workflow automation tools  



Understanding of APIs, webhooks, and SaaS integrations  



Experience in small to mid-sized business environments  



Knowledge of identity and access management frameworks  



 



13. COMPENSATION & EMPLOYMENT DETAILS 



Employment Type: Full-time  



Compensation: $1,500 per month  



Work Arrangement: Remote  



Schedule: Full-time availability during business hours  



Apply Here:

title: Long-Form YouTube Video Editor (Geopolitics Niche)
description:
We’re hiring skilled long-form YouTube video editors for a fast-growing geopolitics channel. You will receive raw footage and are expected to turn it into a high-retention, engaging long-form YouTube video by adding relevant B-roll, clean pacing, and strong storytelling edits.



What You’ll Do:



- Edit long-form YouTube videos in the geopolitics niche (typically 20 minutes)

- Work from raw footage and create a clean, engaging final edit

-Add relevant B-roll (news clips, photos, news screenshots, historical footage, supporting visuals)

- Add basic motion elements when needed (zoom/crop, simple titles, emphasis text)

- Clean up pacing, remove dead air, tighten delivery, and improve flow

- Ensure high-quality audio levels and overall polish



Required Skills:



- High-level English

- Fast communication

- Proven experience editing long-form YouTube videos

- Ability to independently choose and place relevant B-roll to support narration

- Experience in Adobe Premiere Pro OR CapCut (either is fine)



Editing Style Example (Reference)



This is the style we want you to match:

(Example video)



youtu.be/yVG1Xg8zBM0?si=NnFiQRJQ9Bh2OYJ0



If you do not think you can edit the above video, do not apply.



Pay + Work Volume:



- $40 USD per video delivered + bonuses for performance

- We can provide up to 1 video per day

- Expected turnaround time: 24–48 hours per video

- Monthly workload: 10–30 videos per month depending on your speed and quality



Trial Edit (Required - will be 60 seconds in length)



To apply, you will be given a 1-minute intro editing trial.



If your edit matches the style and quality we want, you’ll be selected for ongoing work.



How to Apply (Important)



- Start your application with the word: blueberry. This is to confirm you fully read the job post.



We are looking for long-term editors we can rely on. If you’re fast, detail-oriented, and can make geopolitics content engaging, this will be consistent work.

title: Risk Associate
description:
AJG Transport is a safety focused trucking company committed to running safe, compliant, and efficient operations across the U.S. We are looking for a Risk & Operations Associate to help monitor trucking operations, ensure compliance, and support smooth coordination between drivers and dispatch. This role is ideal for someone detail oriented, responsible, and comfortable working in a fast paced and structured logistics environment.



Role Overview

As a Risk & Operations Associate, you will monitor real time trucking operations to ensure safety, compliance, and continuity by preventing HOS violations, identifying and escalating risks early, maintaining accurate documentation, and supporting either day or night shifts based on business needs.



What You’ll Be Doing

• Monitor safety in trucking operations in real time

• Track Hours of Service (HOS) and ELD compliance

• Identify drivers nearing violations and take proactive action

• Respond to and escalate issues such as delays, a
, equipment concerns, or policy violations

• Conduct basic i
 documentation and reporting

• Log repairs and upload invoices into Loadstop

• Generate simple compliance and audit reports

• Communicate with dispatch, drivers, and leadership as needed

• Ensure clean shift handoffs and clear documentation



What We’re Looking For

• Have experience in US trucking, dispatch, fleet operations, safety, or compliance

• Understand HOS, ELD systems, and FMCSA/DOT standards (strong plus)

• Are comfortable monitoring multiple drivers and assets at once

• Stay calm and organized in a fast paced environment

• Communicate clearly via chat,
, and i
 reports

• Follow SOPs consistently

• Type at least 50 WPM

• Follows instructions consistently and executes with precision

• Team oriented, coachable, and open to feedback without ego



Schedule & Work Setup

• Full Time | Sunday – Thursday 11 PM - 9 AM CDT & Tuesday – Saturday 3 PM - 1 AM CDT

• Must work U.S. business hours

• Collaborative Zoom based work environment (with camera open)

• Time tracking software used for accountability and performance support



Why Join AJG Transport?

• Stable full time opportunity

• Clear responsibilities and structured workflows

• Training and leadership support

• Growth opportunities within operations and safety

• Work directly with US trucking operations



If you’re dependable, detail oriented, and understand the importance of having a safety and monitoring team in a logistics company, recognizing that proactive risk management protects drivers, ensures compliance, and prevents costly disruptions, we’d love to hear from you.

title: Operations Executive / Dispatch Operations Manager (Build & Lead a Dispatch Company)
description:
About Us



We are a U.S.-based towing company operating within the AAA Northeast network as an Independent Service Provider (ISP). Our company handles roadside assistance and towing services and has built a strong reputation for reliability and performance.



We are now launching a dispatching company that will support towing companies and roadside providers across the United States.



We are looking for a high-level operations professional who wants to help build and lead this operation from the ground up.



This is not just a job — this is a long-term leadership opportunity for someone who wants to grow with a fast-scaling company.



Your Role



The first phase of this position will involve learning how our dispatching systems and workflows operate.



Once trained, you will help manage our dispatch operations and oversee a team of dispatchers based in the Philippines.



Over time, you will help build systems, train staff, improve efficiency, and scale the company.



Key Responsibilities



• Learn and master dispatch operations for roadside and towing services

• Assist with managing daily dispatch workflow

• Oversee a team of remote dispatchers in the Philippines

• Monitor response times, service quality, and call handling

• Build dispatch procedures and standard operating systems

• Assist with hiring and training dispatch staff

• Improve operational efficiency and scalability



What We’re Looking For



We are looking for someone who thinks like an operator and problem-solver, not just an employee.



Ideal traits:



• Strong leadership and organizational skills

• Excellent English communication (written and spoken)

• Experience managing remote teams

• Ability to build systems and processes

• Highly reliable and detail-oriented

• Comfortable learning new software platforms



Experience in any of the following is a major advantage:



• Dispatching

• Logistics operations

• Customer service leadership

• Transportation or roadside assistance industries

• Operations management



Work Setup



• Full-time remote position

• Long-term opportunity

• Work directly with the U.S. company owner

• Opportunity to grow into a senior leadership role



Compensation:

Starting salary $640 to $1500 USD

Competitive salary depending on experience, with potential performance bonuses as the company grows.



How to Apply



To apply, please include:

A short introduction about yourself (send 1 min intro video)

Your experience managing operations or teams

A short description of a system or process you helped improve

Your expected monthly salary



We are looking for someone who wants to build something meaningful and grow with the company long term.

title: Paid Media and Client Success Coordinator
description:
We are an established Australian digital marketing agency looking for a smart, reliable, client-focused Paid Media and Client Success Coordinator based in the Philippines.



This is a junior-to-mid-level role, ideal for someone who enjoys both people and performance marketing.



You do not need to be the final strategist for every account. Our leadership team will handle much of the higher-level strategy. What we need is someone who can help manage campaigns well, communicate confidently with clients, work closely with our team, and embrace modern tools, including AI, to improve speed and quality.



If you love being part of a team, building strong client relationships, learning new platforms, and using technology to work smarter, we would love to hear from you.



About the role



You will help manage paid media accounts, support client communication, and work with our internal team to keep campaigns moving smoothly.



You will also be expected to use modern tools, including AI, as part of your workflow. We are looking for someone who is curious, adaptable, and excited by where digital marketing is heading.



What you will be doing



• Support campaign management across Google Ads, Meta Ads, and other platforms where relevant

• Assist with launches, optimisation, audience targeting, creative testing, reporting, and account QA

• Join client calls and communicate with clients in a clear, friendly, confident way

• Build strong relationships with clients and help them feel supported

• Work with our team to action campaign tasks and keep projects organised

• Use AI tools and workflow tools to improve research, execution, reporting, and delivery

• Help manage AI supported implementation workflows, including tools such as Manus and ChatGPT

• Review tracking, conversion actions, landing pages, and campaign structure for accuracy

• Stay open to learning new platforms, systems, and processes as we grow



Platforms you may work across



• Google Ads

• Meta Ads

• LinkedIn Ads

• TikTok Ads

• YouTube

• Microsoft Ads

• Other paid media platforms as needed



You do not need to be an expert in every platform already, but you must have solid paid media fundamentals and be willing to learn.



What we are looking for



• Excellent written and spoken English

• Strong client communication skills

• Friendly, professional, and confident in meetings

• Organised, detail oriented, and reliable

• Comfortable working as part of a team

• Open to feedback, coaching, and continuous improvement

• Comfortable with AI, automation, and modern technology

• Understanding of paid media fundamentals, including objectives, targeting, budgets, tracking, creative testing, and reporting

• Hands on experience with Google Ads and Meta Ads

• Reliable internet, a quiet workspace, and the ability to attend video meetings



This role is a great fit if you are



• Client focused and genuinely enjoy building relationships

• Curious about new technology and excited to use AI well

• Coachable and happy to grow with a team

• Proactive, calm, and solutions focused

• Interested in long term career growth in digital marketing



This role is not a fit if you



• Resist new technology or refuse to use AI tools

• Dislike speaking with clients

• Need constant direction for every task

• Struggle to adapt when platforms and processes change



Why join us



• Long term opportunity with room to grow

• Supportive team environment

• Clear strategic direction from leadership

• Exposure to multiple paid media platforms

• Opportunity to work closely with clients and make a real impact

• Modern, forward thinking agency environment



How to apply



Please send the following:



• A short introduction about yourself

• Your experience with Google Ads, Meta Ads, and any other paid media platforms

• A brief summary of the types of clients or campaigns you have worked on

• A short voice note or video introduction

• Your expected monthly salary in PHP

• Your available work hours

• The words AI Ready at the top of your application so we know you read the full ad



Please also answer these questions:



What paid media platforms have you worked on directly?

What do you enjoy more, client communication, campaign management, or a mix of both?

How have you used AI or automation tools in your work so far?

Tell us about a time you helped improve a client relationship or solved a campaign issue

What interests you about working with a team like ours?



Do not send a generic application. We are looking for someone thoughtful, switched on, and genuinely excited to grow with us.

title: Ads Strategist for E Commerce Brands
description:
We’re an e commerce team testing multiple products every month and we’re looking for an ads strategist to join us long term.



Your role is to build the marketing and advertising strategy behind each product before launch and during scale. We are not looking for someone who only runs ads. We need someone who understands how to find the right angle, build the right campaign structure, guide creatives, and make decisions from data.

• Write briefs for creatives, hooks, offers, and messaging angles

• Analyze ad performance and identify what to cut, improve, retest, or scale

• Improve CPA, CTR, conversion rate, and overall profitability

• Work with the team to constantly test new ideas and new products



What we need from you:

• Strong experience with e commerce paid ads

• Good understanding of Meta ads, testing strategy, and scaling logic

• Strong marketing sense, not just technical ad manager skills

• Ability to read data and turn it into clear action

• Someone proactive, sharp, and serious about performance



Work style:

We test many products every month, so this is long term work with constant volume for the right person.

title: Track & Trace Assistant – Quality Assurance
description:
GH Logistics is a growing freight brokerage and asset based logistics company specializing in dry van and reefer freight. Our team is built on strong systems, clear communication, and reliable execution. As we continue to scale nationally, we are looking for a Track & Trace Assistant – Quality Assurance to help ensure that every load is verified, compliant, and properly tracked before it moves. If you’re someone who notices the small details, enjoys organized processes, and takes pride in preventing problems before they happen this role may be a great fit.



Role Overview

Your work directly prevents missed pickups, tracking gaps, compliance issues, and communication breakdowns while collaborating closely with Operations, Sales, and Management to ensure loads move smoothly and safely with clear SOPs, structured workflows, and strong team support to help you succeed.



What You’ll Be Doing

• Review next day loads before execution

• Verify driver details (name, phone number, reachability)

• Confirm CDL validity and compliance

• Assign and confirm active tracking (FourKites and/or Samsara)

• Call drivers to confirm pickup and delivery details, appointment times, and equipment requirements

• Download and upload customer tenders when applicable

• Ensure load information is accurate in the TMS and QA trackers

• Document findings clearly and consistently

• Escalate issues when something does not meet standards

• Maintain organized records and support process improvements



What Success Looks Like

• All next day loads are reviewed before execution

• Tracking is active and confirmed

• Potential issues are caught early

• Documentation is clear and organized

• Escalations are timely and professional



Who We’re Looking For

• Have experience in US. logistics, freight, tracking, quality assurance, or operational audit

• Are comfortable auditing details and verifying information

• Understand that small details prevent big operational problems

• Follow SOPs consistently

• Communicate clearly with drivers and internal teams

• Stay organized while handling multiple tasks

• Work well independently in a structured remote environment

• Are open to feedback and continuous improvement

• Have strong written and verbal English skills

• Type at least 50 WPM



Work Setup & Schedule

• Full Time | 7:00 AM – 5:00 PM CST or 3 PM - 1 AM CST (San Antonio time)

• Must work U.S. business hours

• Collaborative Zoom based work environment (open camera & unmute mic)

• Time tracking tools used to support accountability



Why Join GH Logistics?

• Clear processes and defined expectations

• Structured systems and documented SOPs

• Supportive team collaboration

• Performance based growth opportunities

• Critical role that directly impacts operational success



If you take pride in doing things right and preventing issues before they happen, we’d love to hear from you.

Job Title: Media Specialist

Location: Remote (Working Hours: 9 AM – 6 PM PST Monday to Friday)



Position Summary

We’re expanding our media team and looking for experienced, results-driven individuals ready to thrive in a fast-paced and dynamic environment. This is a unique opportunity to work closely with our in-house U.S. marketing team and support digital campaigns for some of the biggest car manufacturers in the world.



As a Media Specialist, you will play a key role in executing, monitoring, and optimizing paid media campaigns across Google, Meta 
/Instagram), TikTok, and more. This position offers hands-on exposure to high-budget accounts, advanced campaign strategies, and collaboration with a top-tier team.

We are seeking candidates with at least 3 years of paid media experience who are detail-oriented, accountable, and eager to contribute to a collaborative team.



Key Responsibilities

? Manage and optimize paid media campaigns across platforms (Google, Meta, TikTok, etc.)

? Pull, analyze, and update daily campaign performance reports

? Monitor budgets, pacing, and bidding strategies to ensure efficiency and strong ROI

? Support A/B testing and creative optimization to improve campaign results

? Collaborate with U.S.-based marketing and creative teams

Requirements

? Minimum 3 years of hands-on experience managing paid media campaigns across Meta Ads Manager, Google Ads, TikTok Ads, etc.

? Analytical skills with the ability to interpret data and optimize accordingly

? Proficiency in Google Sheets/Excel for tracking and reporting

? Familiarity with ad tracking (UTM parameters, Meta Pixel, GA4, etc.)

? Reliable internet connection and a quiet, professional workspace

What We Offer

? Opportunity for overtime work and performance-based bonuses

? Training on internal tools, reporting processes, and workflow systems

? Work with a highly experienced U.S. marketing team and major global brands

? Clear path for career growth and skill development within a fast-growing company

Ready to join a high-performing digital media team?

Apply today and bring your skills to an exciting, fast-moving industry.
